---
title: Uptime Kuma
description: Self-hosted uptime monitoring with a status page — a single-service template.
---

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

[Uptime Kuma](https://github.com/louislam/uptime-kuma) is a self-hosted monitoring tool with a fancy
status page. It's the simplest template: a **single service**, no inputs, no secrets.

## What you get

- One service (`uptime-kuma`, the primary) running the `louislam/uptime-kuma` image on port `3001`.
- A persistent volume for its SQLite data, so monitors and history survive restarts.
- The platform's default domain, so it's reachable as soon as it's deployed.

## Deploy it

1. **Create Service → Uptime Kuma** in your environment.
2. There are no inputs to fill in — just create and deploy.
3. Open the default domain shown on the service; the first visit walks you through creating the admin
   account.

<Aside type="note">
	Because it carries a volume, the service uses the `Recreate` rollout strategy (one instance at a time) and can't be horizontally autoscaled —
	expected for a single-node SQLite app.
</Aside>
